name: Git Pushing
description: Automates git stage-commit-push workflow using a smart_commit.sh bash script that analyzes staged files to auto-generate conventional commit messages (feat/fix/docs/test/chore/refactor), adds Claude Code attribution footer, and pushes to remote with branch tracking

findings_summary.notes: Contains SKILL.md and a bash script (scripts/smart_commit.sh). Script reviewed in full: uses `set -e`, color-coded output, checks for empty diffs before staging, runs `git add .` (stages all files), analyzes file names/extensions for commit type detection, builds conventional commit message (type(scope): description), appends Claude Code attribution footer, then pushes. If new branch, uses `git push -u origin <branch>` and prints GitHub PR link. No network calls except git push to configured remote. No secrets handling, no eval, no dynamic code execution. Key concern: `git add .` stages ALL files unconditionally - could accidentally include sensitive files (.env, credentials). Users should ensure .gitignore is properly configured before using this skill. Actual repo star count 406 (recorded as 350 in metadata).

scan_summary: SKILL.md + smart_commit.sh bash script. Script uses `git add .` to stage all files - users must ensure .gitignore excludes sensitive files. Conventional commit message auto-generation, adds Claude Code footer. No other concerns.
